description: Modify `run()` in `evals/overclaw_cluster_grouping/harness.py` so each `per_project` entry is a flat dict with top-level keys: `project_name`, `cluster_analysis`, and every field returned by `score_cluster_analysis(...)` (e.g. `validate_cluster_coverage_passed`, `coverage_score`, `missing_cluster_ids`, `avg_cluster_size`, `weights`, `total_score`). Remove the current shape where those fields live only under `scores`.; Add lightweight output normalization in `run()` before appending each project result: stringify diagnostic collection fields (`missing_cluster_ids`, `duplicate_cluster_ids`, `hallucinated_cluster_ids`, `empty_components`, `empty_descriptions`, `duplicate_names`, `structural_details`, `validate_cluster_coverage_feedback`, `weights`) using a deterministic serializer, and substitute a non-empty placeholder string such as `"none"` when the value would otherwise be empty.

Modify `run()` in `evals/overclaw_cluster_grouping/harness.py` so each `per_project` entry is a single flat dict created by merging `scores` into the project record: include top-level `project_name`, `cluster_analysis`, `validate_cluster_coverage_passed`, `validate_cluster_coverage_feedback`, all score fields, all diagnostic fields, metrics, `weights`, and `total_score`. Do not keep these fields only inside a nested `scores` object.; Add lightweight output normalization inside `run()` before appending each project result: convert diagnostic collections and metadata fields (`validate_cluster_coverage_feedback`, `missing_cluster_ids`, `duplicate_cluster_ids`, `hallucinated_cluster_ids`, `empty_components`, `empty_descriptions`, `duplicate_names`, `structural_details`, `weights`) into deterministic non-empty strings, using a placeholder like `"none"` for empty values, while leaving numeric/boolean fields unchanged.

Update `run()` in `evals/overclaw_cluster_grouping/harness.py` so each element of `per_project` is a flat dict with top-level keys: `project_name`, `cluster_analysis`, and every field returned by `score_cluster_analysis(...)` (`validate_cluster_coverage_passed`, `validate_cluster_coverage_feedback`, `coverage_score`, `duplicate_score`, `hallucinated_score`, `structural_score`, `count_score`, `grouping_score`, `missing_cluster_ids`, `missing_count`, `duplicate_cluster_ids`, `duplicate_count`, `hallucinated_cluster_ids`, `hallucinated_count`, `empty_components`, `empty_descriptions`, `duplicate_names`, `structural_details`, `component_count`, `avg_cluster_size`, `singleton_ratio`, `weights`, `total_score`). Do not leave these fields only under a nested `scores` object.; Add lightweight output normalization inside `run()` before appending each project result: for fields that the evaluator treats as text/non-empty checks (`validate_cluster_coverage_feedback`, `missing_cluster_ids`, `duplicate_cluster_ids`, `hallucinated_cluster_ids`, `empty_components`, `empty_descriptions`, `duplicate_names`, `structural_details`, `weights`, and optionally `cluster_analysis`), serialize lists/dicts deterministically to strings; if the value would be empty, replace it with a non-empty placeholder such as `"none"`. Leave numeric and boolean fields unchanged.
